meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build passive income apps to overcome a lack of portfolio. A professional guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Transform your career by building micro-SaaS products that generate passive income. Instead of traditional resumes, showcase live, revenue-generating tools. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to iterate rapidly, proving to employers you can ship production-grade software that solves actual market problems independently.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank screen of your portfolio site, the cursor blinking in rhythm with your growing anxiety. Every job application asks for years of experience you haven't lived and a portfolio of projects you haven't built. The industry doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about what you can ship to production.

But here is the secret: the most impressive portfolio isn't a collection of tutorials—it's a product that makes money. By pivoting from 'student projects' to 'passive income builders,' you shift the power dynamic. You stop being a job seeker and start being an entrepreneur. Let's build your bridge from 'no experience' to 'proven developer' using the latest AI-native stack.

steps.title: Define a Niche Micro-Utility
steps.description: Identify a small, repetitive task in a specific community (e.g., Discord moderators, Shopify store owners) that can be automated. Focus on high-value, low-complexity problems.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product architect. Suggest 3 micro-SaaS ideas that solve a $50/month recurring revenue problem for niche professional communities. Focus on low-maintenance automation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze these 3 ideas and provide a technical feasibility breakdown for a solo student developer.'

steps.title: Architect the Infrastructure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold a serverless backend. This ensures your app is scalable and costs almost nothing to run while you are learning.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a serverless architecture using Supabase for database and authentication, and Next.js for the frontend. Include Stripe integration for subscription management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project folder and install dependencies for Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe SDK.'

steps.title: Rapid Feature Development
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to write your core business logic. Focus on the 'happy path'—the exact sequence of events that results in a user payment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the core business logic for a subscription-based automation tool. Focus on security and clean API routing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create the Stripe webhook handler to manage subscription status updates in the Supabase users table.'

steps.title: Automated Testing and QA
steps.description: Before launching, use AI-driven testing to ensure your app doesn't break under load. This demonstrates professional rigor to potential employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate comprehensive unit tests for the authentication and payment flows. Use Jest and React Testing Library.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run the test suite and fix any identified regressions in the API routes.'

steps.title: Production Deployment
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel or Railway. A live URL is worth 1,000 lines of GitHub code. This is your primary portfolio asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for production-ready deployment, including environment variable management and domain configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure production environment variables and prepare the deployment manifest.'

pro_tips: Focus on 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over feature richness; a simple tool that solves one problem is better than a complex app that does nothing well.
Document your failures in a 'Build Log' GitHub repository; employers value the debugging process more than the final code.
Use Stripe's 'Test Mode' to simulate edge cases like failed payments or subscription cancellations to prove your error handling.
Optimize your landing page for conversion; even if you don't get 100 users, showing you understand the funnel is a massive resume booster.
Keep your tech stack lean; using too many experimental libraries looks like 'resume-driven development' rather than 'problem-solving development'.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, write a technical blog post detailing the architecture, the biggest bug you solved, and your revenue goals. Link this post directly on your resume.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a live product. Use this momentum to land your dream role or scale your side hustle into a career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
